components.evidence: 20 mM glucose, 40 mM glycerol, or 24 mM xylose as sole carbon source

media.base_medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) of Hartmans et al. 1989 (Appl. Environ. Microbiol. 55:2850-2855), as used for Pseudomonas taiwanensis; here with 5-fold phosphate buffer and 2-fold mineral-salts stock.

sources_chased.what: target paper main text (PMC6882275) - modifications to Hartmans MSM (2x mineral salts, 5x phosphate)
base Hartmans 1989 MSM full composition (missing EDTA chelator, (NH4)2SO4 amount, 20 mM glucose baseline)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch Frontiers fbioe.2019.00130 and fbioe.2019.00312 (same Blank/Schmid group, verbatim MSM recipe per Hartmans 1989); original Hartmans 1989 AEM article not directly retrievable but recipe cross-confirmed across 3 same-lineage sources

media.notes: MSM for Pseudomonas taiwanensis grown aerobically in shake flasks / well plates at pH 7.0. Union recipe = Hartmans 1989 base + this paper's 5x phosphate buffer + 2x mineral-salts stock + 20 mM glucose. The paper's explicit 2x list omits EDTA; EDTA is recovered from the base recipe at its 1x value of 10 mg/L (whether it was also doubled is not stated). Note: the current model exchange set carried a spurious Fe3+ (EX_fe3_e) - the actual medium supplies only ferrous iron as FeSO4.7H2O; oxygen corrected from 'facultative' to aerobic.
